summary: Iteration 3 LMS testing — 22 new backend tests for Assignments, File upload/download (Emergent object storage), Submission + grading with GRADE notification side-effect, Socket.IO handshake (HTTP 200 polling), and Email service mock-fallback when RESEND_API_KEY is empty. 21 PASS / 1 SKIP (no free published course to enroll). Backend success: 100% of executed cases. Frontend smoke: Notifications page lists fresh 'Assignment graded: TEST_Assignment iter3 v2' entries (50/50 and 45/50) confirming end-to-end create_notification → list_notifications integration. Educator NotificationBell present and clickable on Sarah's navbar. Resend mocking confirmed — register / forgot-password / enroll do NOT 500 with empty key.

minor.issue: 20MB size limit is enforced server-side; we did not actually upload a 20MB file (CI / bandwidth). Limit verified via code review (lines 1502-1504 of server.py).
No file_urls validation (e.g., max count, that they point to /api/files/...). Caller could pass arbitrary URLs. Low risk since students must be enrolled.

When upsert overwrites max_score, any already-graded submissions are NOT re-clamped — historical scores can exceed new max_score (we observed 92/100 then upserted to max=50; existing 92/100 grade persists). Worth a note in admin docs.

ui_bugs.issue: Playwright found 0 buttons matching [data-testid^='build-assignment-'] in CourseBuilder of one course (6a16a2b30c3d7be8a18b1522). Code shows the button exists per-lesson (line 222) but sections may need to be expanded first. Recommendation: expand sections by default OR add a 'Manage Assignments' shortcut.

integration_issues.issue: Could not visually verify CTA because Alex's enrolled course (Python for Data Science Bootcamp) had no ASSIGNMENT lessons in this session. The Modern React assignment created by iter3 is on Sarah's first course; Alex's enrollment status there was verified server-side (idempotent submission worked) but the UI walkthrough for the CTA wasn't reachable from the player I opened. Main agent can verify directly by visiting /learn/<modern-react-slug> as Alex.

action_items: Optional UX: auto-expand the first section in CourseBuilder so the per-lesson '+ Assign.' button is visible without an extra click.
Consider re-clamping (or flagging) historical submission scores when an educator lowers max_score via /api/assignments POST upsert.
Validate file_urls payload on POST /api/assignments/{id}/submit (e.g., shape, ownership, prefix '/api/files/').

Optional: write a Playwright e2e for the live Socket.IO push (two browser contexts: grade from Sarah, observe toast in Alex's open tab) — handshake passes but real push wasn't verified live in this run, only via polled /api/notifications which DID show new GRADE entries.
Seed: TEST_iter3_*@example.com users + TEST_Assignment iter3 v2 assignment + multiple TEST_Assignment iter3 v2 graded notifications were created during this run. Cleanup recommended.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1682 lines — past the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers (auth/courses/assignments/notifications/files/admin) for maintainability.
create_notification() correctly wraps insert + push (server.py line 55-69) — good design. However it stores `user_id` as ObjectId but push_notification stringifies — confirmed working.

/api/files/{full_path:path} endpoint is fully PUBLIC (no auth) — any authenticated user uploads can be downloaded by anyone who knows the path. Consider signing URLs or requiring auth on download for sensitive submission files.
AssignmentIn / SubmissionTextIn / GradeIn — submit endpoint uses raw `body: dict` instead of Pydantic SubmissionTextIn model (server.py line 1532). This skips validation; consider using the model.
Socket.IO connect event allows anonymous connection (returns True even without token). This is intentional for graceful UI but means rooms have no fallback identity for anonymous users — fine.
RESEND_API_KEY empty path correctly returns {status:'mocked', mocked:True} — verified via register/forgot/enroll smoke tests; no 500s.
Idempotency of submit() and upsert() of create_assignment() both confirmed by tests (same _id reused).
Grade clamp logic correctly bounds score to [0, max_score] (server.py line 1591) — verified by test_grade_clamps_to_max_score.

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 executed; 1 skipped for missing free course)
success_rate.frontend: ~90% (Notifications + AssignmentBuilder verified; CourseBuilder '+ Assign.' button not discovered due to collapsed sections; AssignmentView/CoursePlayer CTA not visually traversed because Alex's open course had no ASSIGNMENT lessons)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_iter3_<rand>@example.com student accounts during register-no-500 test (not deleted). TEST_Assignment iter3 v2 assignment overwrote whatever was on Sarah's first course's first lesson and marked that lesson as type=ASSIGNMENT (recovery: re-seed). Multiple Assignment-Graded notifications for Alex (50/50, 45/50, 50/50, 50/50 — visible in /dashboard/notifications). Files uploaded for Alex into Emergent object storage (small text payloads).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration-3 backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py (22 cases). Module-scoped fixtures depend on env var REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — must export before invoking pytest (the default fallback uses the same URL but is brittle). Test_iteration3 uses Sarah's first course / first lesson — it will *upsert* whatever assignment exists there to title 'TEST_Assignment iter3 v2', max_score=50. Frontend confirmed: NotificationBell present in Navbar + DashboardLayout, Notifications page renders graded notifications in real time on next poll/load. Socket.IO handshake at /socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=polling returns 200 with sid — live push not verified via 2-tab e2e in this run.
rca of the issue: No bugs blocking iteration sign-off. CourseBuilder '+ Assign.' button non-discovery is almost certainly a collapsed-section UX (button exists in DOM only when section is expanded). The assignment grading → notification → UI propagation works fully (verified via polled GET /api/notifications and visible in the screenshot).
